content:
# Grading Pipeline Perf v3 — Decision Record (2026-04-02)
## What: Four performance optimizations for dorea grade targeting ~12 min savings on 1671-frame 4K clips
## Key Design Decisions:
1. Raw RGB IPC replaces PNG encode/decode — base64 overhead remains (~33%) but PNG construction/parsing eliminated. Binary IPC protocol deferred as future optimization.
2. HF processor bypass with hardcoded ImageNet normalization — CUDA is now a hard requirement (no silent CPU fallback). Preview command noted as needing separate handling.
3. Fused LAB pass — single sRGB↔LAB roundtrip instead of two. Not bit-exact (accepted ΔE < 2 tolerance, tested at 0.05 normalized RGB). Parallelized with rayon par_chunks_exact_mut.
4. Temporal grade interpolation — MSE-based keyframe detection at proxy resolution. Non-keyframe frames interpolate graded RGB output (not depth). Scene cuts flush buffer without forward interpolation.
5. --cpu-only flag now errors early for dorea grade (GPU required). Flag retained for other commands.
## Review Findings Worth Remembering:
- ptr::eq on Vec heap pointers works for detecting same-allocation but is fragile — use Option<&T> instead
- np.frombuffer returns read-only arrays — always .copy() when downstream code might mutate
- Fused ambiance duplicates ~80 lines of grading logic — extract shared grade_pixel_lab() in future
- lerp_graded allocates per non-keyframe (~35GB transient at 4K) — pre-allocated scratch buffer is a follow-up
## What Blocked / Surprised:
- gh CLI defaulted to corvia-workspace upstream instead of dorea-workspace origin — required gh repo set-default fix
- dorea code repo (repos/dorea/) is gitignored by workspace repo — separate git workflows needed
